_meta.purpose: Frozen config for LSTM+RF v2 baseline (F1=0.4747) — source of truth for Experiments 1 (no-velocity) and 2 (with-velocity)
_meta.framework: Keras / TensorFlow (NOT PyTorch) — see critical_blocker_1 in red_flags

variant: Hybrid v2: LSTM sigmoid probability + RF

sequence_construction.method: per-cardholder (cc_num), sorted by trans_date_trans_time ascending, sliding window including current transaction
sequence_construction.padding: zeros prepended when card history < seq_len
sequence_construction.cc_num_reattachment: positional .values from fraudTrain.csv / fraudTest.csv — assumes row order preserved between original and engineered CSVs

experiment_mapping_note: Because the F1=0.4747 run already includes velocity features, Experiment 2 (LSTM_with_velocity) is effectively a reproducibility re-run of v2. Experiment 1 (LSTM_no_velocity) is the genuinely new run. This must be disclosed in the viva.

rf_input.composition: 1 LSTM sigmoid probability + static (current transaction) features

preprocessing.fit: fit_transform on X_train[feature_cols] only (no leakage at this step)

random_seeds.tensorflow_seed: NOT SET IN ORIGINAL — must set to 42 for reruns
random_seeds.numpy_seed: NOT SET IN ORIGINAL — must set to 42 for reruns
random_seeds.python_random_seed: NOT SET IN ORIGINAL — must set to 42 for reruns

red_flags: CRITICAL BLOCKER: LSTM is Keras/TensorFlow, not PyTorch. The torch+cu121 venv plan will not help LSTM runs. Windows native TensorFlow GPU support ended at TF 2.10; WSL2 or tensorflow-directml are the viable GPU paths. Simplest fallback: run LSTM on CPU (Keras works fine on CPU in Python 3.14).
LSTM is non-deterministic (no TF/numpy/python seed set in original). Exact F1=0.4747 is not bit-reproducible; expect ±0.01-0.03 drift even with correct seeds.
Keras validation_split takes the tail 10% of the already-cc_num-sorted training array — validation set is skewed toward the highest cc_num cards. Poor protocol but not a data leak.

Velocity features (velocity_1h, velocity_24h, amount_velocity_1h) are consumed from engineered CSVs produced by EDA_FYP_FINAL.ipynb. If those were computed on train+test combined, there is upstream leakage. Hybrid notebook itself does not recompute them. WORTH VERIFYING before publishing results.
Sequence padding with zeros on scaled data (post StandardScaler) — padded rows look like 'mean' transactions. Minor concern.
cc_num positional re-attachment from original CSVs to engineered CSVs assumes row order is preserved. If EDA notebook reordered rows, cc_num aligns to wrong feature vectors silently. WORTH VERIFYING.
Colab Drive paths hardcoded in cells 0-4 (`/content/drive/MyDrive/FYP_Fraud_Detection/`). Must be rewritten for local execution.
RF uses class_weight={0:1, 1:150} while LSTM uses {0:1, 1:171.8}. Inconsistency — likely hand-tuned for RF. Reproduce as-is.
blockers_to_deterministic_rerun: No TF/numpy/python seed → set all before rerun
Colab paths → rewrite to local (same folder as notebook)
TF op-level nondeterminism on GPU → set TF_DETERMINISTIC_OPS=1 (irrelevant if running on CPU)
Upstream engineered CSV row alignment → verify or recompute velocity features from scratch
